introduction: This spec defines the workspace management capabilities of af-hub, the coordination hub for the agent-fox platform. Workspaces are git-repo-scoped execution contexts owned by users and optionally associated with organizations; af-hub manages their lifecycle, access control, storage, API endpoints, and CLI commands on top of the identity and infrastructure provided by apikit.

glossary.APIGroup: The apikit server method that returns the Echo router group for API endpoints. Signature: func (s *Server) APIGroup() *echo.Group.
glossary.Bootstrap: The apikit function that bootstraps the server. Signature: func Bootstrap(ctx context.Context, database *DB, opts BootstrapOptions) error.
glossary.CLIExecute: The apikit function that executes the CLI command tree. Signature: func CLIExecute() error.
glossary.CLIExitCode: The apikit function that converts an error to a CLI exit code. Signature: func CLIExitCode(err error) int.
glossary.CLIPrintError: The apikit function that prints a CLI error to stderr. Signature: func CLIPrintError(err error).
glossary.HealthChecker: An apikit interface implemented by hub using a database ping to report server health.
glossary.LoadConfig: The apikit function that loads server configuration. Signature: func LoadConfig() (*Config, error).
glossary.MountHandlers: The apikit server method that registers route handlers and permission scopes. Signature: func (s *Server) MountHandlers(database *DB, permissions ...Permission) error.
glossary.NewServer: The apikit function that creates a new server instance. Signature: func NewServer(cfg *Config, checker HealthChecker) *Server.
glossary.OpenDatabase: The apikit function that opens the SQLite database. Signature: func OpenDatabase(path string) (*DB, error).
glossary.Organization: An apikit-managed grouping of users. Workspaces can optionally be associated with an organization via org_id.
glossary.PAT: Personal Access Token. An apikit credential type with fine-grained permission scopes. Hub extends the permission registry with workspace-specific scopes.
glossary.Permission: An apikit type used to register permission scopes. Signature: type Permission struct { Resource, Action string }.
glossary.Physical delete: Removal of a database row entirely. Used for workspace deletion — the slug is freed for reuse and no audit record is retained.
glossary.RootCommand: The apikit function that returns the root cobra.Command embedding all standard apikit CLI subcommands. Signature: func RootCommand() *cobra.Command.
glossary.Slug: A globally unique, URL-safe identifier for a workspace. Lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens, 3–64 chars, starts with a letter, no trailing hyphen, no consecutive hyphens.
glossary.Workspace: A git-repo-scoped execution context where work is done. Maps to one git repository and optional branch. Owned by a user, optionally associated with an organization.
glossary.active: The default workspace lifecycle state. The workspace is fully operational.
glossary.afc: The agent-fox CLI binary that embeds apikit commands and adds workspace-specific subcommands under 'afc workspace'.
glossary.apikit: The foundation library (github.com/txsvc/apikit) that provides server bootstrap, authentication, OAuth, user management, organization management, API keys, PATs, and CLI infrastructure.
glossary.archived: A workspace lifecycle state in which the workspace is read-only and hidden from default listings but all state is preserved.
glossary.branch: An optional git ref name specifying which branch of the repository the workspace targets. Null means the repository's default branch.
glossary.created_at: An RFC 3339 timestamp string stored in the workspaces table recording when the workspace was created.
glossary.git_url: The HTTPS or SSH URL of the git repository associated with a workspace, stored as metadata.
glossary.include_archived: A query parameter (?include_archived=true) for the list workspaces endpoint that causes archived workspaces to be included in results.
glossary.org_id: An optional UUID referencing an apikit-managed organization associated with a workspace.
glossary.owner_id: The user ID of the user who created and owns a workspace.
glossary.status: The lifecycle state of a workspace: active, archived. Deleted workspaces are physically removed and never written with the value 'deleted'.
glossary.updated_at: An RFC 3339 timestamp string stored in the workspaces table recording when the workspace was last modified.
glossary.workspaces table: The SQLite table managed by af-hub that stores workspace records with columns: slug, git_url, branch, owner_id, org_id, status, created_at, updated_at.
glossary.workspaces:create: A PAT permission scope granting the ability to create workspaces on behalf of the PAT's owner, and to read own workspaces.
glossary.workspaces:read: A PAT permission scope granting read-only access to list and view workspaces owned by the PAT's owner.

requirements.title: Workspace data model and field validation

user_story.goal: define a workspace with a unique slug, git_url, optional branch, owner_id, optional org_id, and status
user_story.benefit: every workspace is unambiguously identified and associated with exactly one git repository

acceptance_criteria.action: stores slug, git_url, branch (nullable), owner_id, org_id (nullable), status, created_at, and updated_at in the workspaces table
accepts slugs that are 3–64 characters, lowercase alphanumeric plus hyphens, start with a letter, have no trailing hyphen, and have no consecutive hyphens
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: validation passes; slug is accepted

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the slug
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: slug fails format, length, leading-character, trailing-hyphen, or consecutive-hyphen rules

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts git_url values matching HTTPS format (https://<host>/<path>) or SSH format (git@<host>:<path>) where both host and path are non-empty; validates by pattern matching only without DNS resolution or protocol negotiation
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: validation passes; git_url is accepted

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the git_url
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: git_url is empty, uses plain HTTP, has an empty host, or has an empty path segment

acceptance_criteria.action: accepts branch values that are non-empty and follow git ref naming rules: no ASCII control characters, no space, no ~, ^, :, ?, *, [, or \, no .. sequences, no trailing .lock, no trailing dot, no leading dot in any path component
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: validation passes; branch is accepted

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the branch value
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: branch is provided but violates any git ref naming rule

acceptance_criteria.action: allows the same git_url to appear in multiple workspaces with different slugs
stores status as the string 'active' or 'archived' in the workspaces table; never writes the value 'deleted' because deleted workspaces are physically removed
stores created_at and updated_at as RFC 3339 strings in the workspaces table

edge_cases.action: rejects the slug as out of range
edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
edge_cases.error_condition: slug is exactly 2 characters (below minimum) or exactly 65 characters (above maximum)

edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
edge_cases.error_condition: slug starts with a digit or hyphen instead of a letter

edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
edge_cases.error_condition: branch is provided as an empty string

edge_cases.action: rejects the URL as invalid format
edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}

user_story.goal: have the workspaces table created automatically on server boot
user_story.benefit: no manual schema setup is required before the server accepts requests

acceptance_criteria.action: executes CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for the workspaces table with columns slug (TEXT PRIMARY KEY), git_url (TEXT NOT NULL), branch (TEXT), owner_id (TEXT NOT NULL), org_id (TEXT), status (TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active'), created_at (TEXT NOT NULL), updated_at (TEXT NOT NULL), using the *apikit.DB abstraction
proceeds without error due to IF NOT EXISTS semantics
acceptance_criteria.trigger: workspaces table already exists at boot

edge_cases.action: logs the error and returns a non-nil error from the boot sequence so the process exits rather than serving requests against an uninitialized schema
edge_cases.return_contract: boot function returns a non-nil error; server does not start
edge_cases.error_condition: database is unavailable or the CREATE TABLE statement fails during boot

user_story.goal: register workspace permission scopes with apikit's PAT permission registry
user_story.benefit: PATs can be scoped to workspace operations without custom credential types

acceptance_criteria.action: registers the Permission{Resource: "workspaces", Action: "read"} scope (workspaces:read) with apikit's permission registry
registers the Permission{Resource: "workspaces", Action: "create"} scope (workspaces:create) with apikit's permission registry
grants read-only access to list and view the workspaces owned by the PAT's owner, equivalent to what the owner could read with their API key
grants the ability to create workspaces on behalf of the PAT's owner and grants read access to the owner's own workspaces

user_story.goal: ensure only authorized credentials can access or modify my workspaces
user_story.benefit: workspaces are protected from unauthorized access or modification

acceptance_criteria.action: grants admin tokens full read and write access to all workspaces except workspace creation
rejects the request because workspaces require a real user as owner
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: admin token is used to create a workspace

acceptance_criteria.action: grants user API key holders full access to their own workspaces and the ability to create workspaces
grants PAT with workspaces:read read-only access to the PAT owner's workspaces only
grants PAT with workspaces:create the ability to create workspaces on behalf of the owner and read the owner's own workspaces
returns 404 rather than 403 to prevent slug enumeration
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 404 with JSON body {"error":{"code":404,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: a workspace exists but the requesting credential does not have access to it

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request as unauthenticated
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 401 with JSON body {"error":{"code":401,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: request has missing, invalid, or expired credential

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request as forbidden
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: credential is valid but does not have the required permission scope for the requested operation

edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
edge_cases.error_condition: PAT with workspaces:read attempts to archive, reactivate, or delete a workspace

edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
edge_cases.error_condition: PAT with workspaces:create attempts to archive, reactivate, or delete a workspace

user_story.goal: register a new workspace by providing a slug, git_url, optional branch, and optional org_id
user_story.benefit: I have a tracked execution context mapped to my git repository

acceptance_criteria.action: creates the workspace record in the workspaces table with status active, sets owner_id to the creating user's ID, sets created_at and updated_at to the current UTC time as RFC 3339 strings, and returns the workspace object
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 201 with JSON workspace object: {slug, git_url, branch, owner_id, org_id, status:"active", created_at, updated_at}
acceptance_criteria.trigger: authenticated user API key or PAT with workspaces:create submits a valid create workspace request

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with a conflict error
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 409 with JSON body {"error":{"code":409,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: slug is already used by an existing workspace

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: org_id is provided but the creating user is not a member of that organization

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: org_id is provided but does not reference an existing organization

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: request body is missing or malformed

acceptance_criteria.action: stores the branch value in the workspaces table
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returned workspace object includes branch field with the provided value
acceptance_criteria.trigger: request includes a valid optional branch

acceptance_criteria.action: stores NULL for branch in the workspaces table
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returned workspace object includes branch: null

edge_cases.action: rejects the request because workspaces require a real user as owner
edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
edge_cases.error_condition: admin token is used to call the create workspace endpoint

edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
edge_cases.error_condition: PAT with workspaces:read (no workspaces:create) attempts to create a workspace

edge_cases.action: returns an error to the caller; no partial workspace record is retained
edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 500 with JSON body {"error":{"code":500,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
edge_cases.error_condition: database insert fails due to a transient error after validation passes

user_story.goal: retrieve a list of workspaces I have access to
user_story.benefit: I can see all my workspaces in one call

acceptance_criteria.action: returns all workspaces across all users, excluding archived workspaces by default, ordered by created_at descending
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 200 with JSON array of workspace objects; empty array when none match

acceptance_criteria.action: returns only the workspaces owned by the authenticated user, excluding archived workspaces by default, ordered by created_at descending
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 200 with JSON array of workspace objects; empty array when none match
acceptance_criteria.trigger: user API key or PAT with workspaces:read or workspaces:create makes the request

acceptance_criteria.action: includes archived workspaces in the result set in addition to active workspaces
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 200 with JSON array including both active and archived workspace objects

acceptance_criteria.action: returns an empty array
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 200 with JSON body []
acceptance_criteria.trigger: no workspaces match the query criteria

edge_cases.action: returns results without pagination; the full matching set is returned in a single response
edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 200 with complete JSON array; no pagination headers or cursors

user_story.goal: fetch details of a specific workspace by its slug
user_story.benefit: I can inspect the full state of a workspace I have access to

acceptance_criteria.action: retrieves the workspace record from the workspaces table and returns it
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 200 with JSON workspace object
acceptance_criteria.trigger: authorized credential requests a workspace by slug

acceptance_criteria.action: returns not found
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 404 with JSON body {"error":{"code":404,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: no workspace exists with the given slug

acceptance_criteria.action: returns not found to prevent slug enumeration
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 404 with JSON body {"error":{"code":404,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: workspace exists but the requesting credential does not own it and is not an admin

user_story.goal: archive a workspace to make it read-only and hide it from default listings
user_story.benefit: I can preserve workspace state without it cluttering active workspace views

acceptance_criteria.action: updates the workspace status to 'archived' in the workspaces table, sets updated_at to current time, and returns the updated workspace object
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 200 with JSON workspace object where status is "archived"
acceptance_criteria.trigger: workspace owner or admin sends an archive request for an active workspace

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the transition as an error, not a no-op
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: workspace is already archived

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request as forbidden
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: requesting credential is a PAT (workspaces:read or workspaces:create) and not the owner API key or admin

acceptance_criteria.action: returns not found
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 404 with JSON body {"error":{"code":404,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: workspace does not exist or credential cannot access it

user_story.goal: reactivate an archived workspace to restore it to active status
user_story.benefit: I can resume work in a previously archived workspace without losing any state

acceptance_criteria.action: updates the workspace status to 'active' in the workspaces table, sets updated_at to current time, and returns the updated workspace object
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 200 with JSON workspace object where status is "active"
acceptance_criteria.trigger: workspace owner or admin sends a reactivate request for an archived workspace

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the transition as an error, not a no-op
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: workspace is already active

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request as forbidden
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: requesting credential is a PAT and not the owner API key or admin

acceptance_criteria.action: returns not found
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 404 with JSON body {"error":{"code":404,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: workspace does not exist or credential cannot access it

user_story.benefit: I can free the slug for reuse and remove stale workspace records

acceptance_criteria.action: physically removes the workspace row from the workspaces table; the slug becomes available for reuse immediately
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 204 with empty body
acceptance_criteria.trigger: workspace owner or admin sends a delete request for an archived workspace

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the delete request; only archived workspaces can be deleted
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 400 with JSON body {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: workspace is active (not archived)

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request as forbidden
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 403 with JSON body {"error":{"code":403,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: requesting credential is a PAT and not the owner API key or admin

acceptance_criteria.action: returns not found
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: returns HTTP 404 with JSON body {"error":{"code":404,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: workspace does not exist or credential cannot access it

acceptance_criteria.action: performs a physical delete by removing the row from the workspaces table; no soft-delete flag or audit record is retained

edge_cases.action: returns an error to the caller; workspace row is not guaranteed removed
edge_cases.return_contract: returns HTTP 500 with JSON body {"error":{"code":500,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}
edge_cases.error_condition: database DELETE statement fails after authorization passes

user_story.goal: register a workspace from the CLI using --git-url and --slug flags
user_story.benefit: I can create workspaces without writing API calls directly

acceptance_criteria.action: calls POST /api/v1/workspaces and prints the returned workspace object as JSON to stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 0; prints JSON workspace object to stdout
acceptance_criteria.trigger: user runs 'afc workspace create --git-url <url> --slug <slug>' with valid flags

acceptance_criteria.action: resolves the org slug to a UUID before calling the API; passes org_id in the request body
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 0 on success; workspace object includes org_id

acceptance_criteria.action: prints a usage hint to stderr and exits without making an API call
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 1; usage hint printed to stderr; no API call made
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: --git-url or --slug flag is missing

acceptance_criteria.action: prints an error message to stderr and exits without making the workspace create API call
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 1; error printed to stderr; no workspace create API call made
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: --org slug cannot be resolved (org not found or API error)

acceptance_criteria.action: prints the error to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 1; error message printed to stderr
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: API call returns an error

edge_cases.action: treats the response as a resolution failure and prints an error to stderr without making the workspace create API call
edge_cases.error_condition: org slug resolution API call returns a success status but with a missing or null org UUID field

edge_cases.action: treats the response as a resolution failure and prints an error to stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: org slug resolution API call returns an unexpected data shape

user_story.goal: list my workspaces from the CLI
user_story.benefit: I can quickly see all my workspaces without using the API directly

acceptance_criteria.action: calls GET /api/v1/workspaces and prints the resulting JSON array to stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 0; prints JSON array to stdout

acceptance_criteria.action: passes ?include_archived=true to GET /api/v1/workspaces
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 0; response includes archived workspaces; prints JSON array to stdout

acceptance_criteria.action: prints the error to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 1; error message printed to stderr
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: API call returns an error

user_story.goal: fetch details of a specific workspace by slug from the CLI
user_story.benefit: I can inspect a workspace's current state without using the API directly

acceptance_criteria.action: calls GET /api/v1/workspaces/:slug and prints the workspace object as JSON to stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 0; prints JSON workspace object to stdout

acceptance_criteria.action: prints the error to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 1; error message printed to stderr
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: API call returns an error (e.g. 404 or 401)

user_story.goal: archive a workspace from the CLI
user_story.benefit: I can preserve a workspace's state and hide it from default listings without writing API calls

acceptance_criteria.action: calls POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/archive and prints the updated workspace object as JSON to stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 0; prints JSON workspace object with status "archived" to stdout

acceptance_criteria.action: prints the error to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 1; error message printed to stderr
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: API call returns an error

user_story.goal: reactivate an archived workspace from the CLI
user_story.benefit: I can resume work in an archived workspace without writing API calls

acceptance_criteria.action: calls POST /api/v1/workspaces/:slug/reactivate and prints the updated workspace object as JSON to stdout
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 0; prints JSON workspace object with status "active" to stdout

acceptance_criteria.action: prints the error to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 1; error message printed to stderr
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: API call returns an error

user_story.goal: permanently delete an archived workspace from the CLI with an explicit confirmation flag
user_story.benefit: I can clean up archived workspaces safely without risking accidental deletion

acceptance_criteria.action: calls DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:slug and prints a confirmation message to stderr on success
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 0; confirmation message printed to stderr

acceptance_criteria.action: prints a usage hint to stderr and exits without making an API call
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 1; usage hint printed to stderr; no API call made
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: --confirm flag is omitted

acceptance_criteria.action: prints the error to stderr
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 1; error message printed to stderr
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: API call returns an error

requirements.title: CLI command tree integration with apikit

user_story.goal: embed all apikit standard commands in the afc CLI alongside workspace commands
user_story.benefit: users have a unified CLI with both platform management and workspace commands

acceptance_criteria.action: builds the command tree by calling RootCommand() and adding workspace subcommands under the 'workspace' group; includes LoginCmd(), UserCmd(), KeysCmd(), TokensCmd(), OrgsCmd(), and AdminCmd() as registered subcommands
uses CLIExecute() to execute the command tree, CLIPrintError() to print errors to stderr, and CLIExitCode() to derive the process exit code
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: exits 0 on success, 1 on any error

acceptance_criteria.action: signals all errors via return values using the (*T, error) Go idiom; library functions never call os.Exit directly

user_story.goal: receive a consistent JSON error envelope for all failure responses
user_story.benefit: I can parse errors programmatically without special-casing each endpoint

acceptance_criteria.action: formats all error responses using apikit's standard JSON envelope: {"error":{"code":<HTTP_STATUS>,"message":"Human-readable description"}}
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: JSON body with keys 'error.code' (integer) and 'error.message' (string)

correctness_properties.title: Slug global uniqueness invariant
correctness_properties.for_any: workspace record in the workspaces table
correctness_properties.invariant: the slug column value is unique across all rows; no two workspace records share the same slug at any point in time

correctness_properties.title: Owner is always a real user invariant
correctness_properties.for_any: workspace record in the workspaces table
correctness_properties.invariant: the owner_id column is non-null and references a real user; no workspace record exists with an admin-token identity as its owner

correctness_properties.title: Valid status values invariant
correctness_properties.for_any: workspace record in the workspaces table
correctness_properties.invariant: the status column contains exactly 'active' or 'archived'; the value 'deleted' is never written because deleted workspaces are physically removed

correctness_properties.title: Delete-only-from-archived invariant
correctness_properties.invariant: a workspace row is physically removed only when its status is 'archived' at the time of deletion; deletion of an active workspace is always rejected

correctness_properties.title: Access scoping invariant
correctness_properties.for_any: GET /api/v1/workspaces response for a non-admin credential
correctness_properties.invariant: the returned workspace objects contain only workspaces whose owner_id matches the authenticated user's ID; workspaces belonging to other users are never included

correctness_properties.title: Archived exclusion from default listing invariant
correctness_properties.for_any: GET /api/v1/workspaces response when include_archived query parameter is absent or false
correctness_properties.invariant: no workspace object with status 'archived' appears in the response array

correctness_properties.title: RFC 3339 timestamp invariant
correctness_properties.for_any: workspace record in the workspaces table
correctness_properties.invariant: the created_at and updated_at columns always contain valid RFC 3339 timestamp strings; they are never null or empty

correctness_properties.title: Slug enumeration prevention invariant
correctness_properties.for_any: request for a workspace the credential cannot access
correctness_properties.invariant: the API returns HTTP 404 regardless of whether the workspace exists; HTTP 403 is never returned for workspace existence checks to prevent slug enumeration

steps.action: runs 'afc workspace create --git-url https://github.com/org/repo --slug my-workspace'
validates flags are present, then calls POST /api/v1/workspaces with slug, git_url, and owner credentials
validates the user API key and injects the authenticated user identity into the request context
validates slug format, git_url format, and (if provided) branch and org_id; checks slug uniqueness in the workspaces table
inserts a new row into the workspaces table with status 'active', owner_id set to the authenticated user, and RFC 3339 created_at/updated_at timestamps
returns HTTP 201 with the workspace JSON object
prints the workspace JSON object to stdout and exits 0

execution_paths.title: Archive then delete workspace — happy path

steps.action: runs 'afc workspace archive my-workspace'
calls POST /api/v1/workspaces/my-workspace/archive with owner credentials
validates the user API key and confirms the user is the workspace owner
confirms workspace status is 'active', updates status to 'archived' in the workspaces table, sets updated_at
returns HTTP 200 with the updated workspace JSON object (status: archived)
prints the workspace JSON to stdout and exits 0
runs 'afc workspace delete my-workspace --confirm'
confirms --confirm flag is present, then calls DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/my-workspace
confirms workspace status is 'archived', physically removes the row from the workspaces table
returns HTTP 204 with empty body
prints confirmation message to stderr and exits 0

execution_paths.title: List workspaces with archived included — happy path

steps.action: runs 'afc workspace list --include-archived'
calls GET /api/v1/workspaces?include_archived=true with user credentials
validates the credential and resolves the requesting user identity
queries the workspaces table for all workspaces owned by the authenticated user (both active and archived), ordered by created_at descending
returns HTTP 200 with a JSON array of workspace objects
prints the JSON array to stdout and exits 0

execution_paths.title: Create workspace with org association — org membership check

steps.action: runs 'afc workspace create --git-url git@github.com:org/repo --slug team-ws --org my-org'
resolves the org slug 'my-org' to a UUID via the apikit SDK or a direct API call
calls POST /api/v1/workspaces with slug, git_url, and org_id (resolved UUID)
validates the user API key and injects the authenticated user identity
verifies the authenticated user is a member of the organization referenced by org_id
inserts workspace row with org_id set and returns HTTP 201 with the workspace JSON object
prints the workspace JSON to stdout and exits 0

execution_paths.title: PAT with workspaces:read reads own workspaces

steps.action: calls GET /api/v1/workspaces with a PAT carrying workspaces:read scope
validates the PAT, resolves the PAT owner, and confirms the workspaces:read scope is present
scopes the query to workspaces owned by the PAT's owner, excluding archived workspaces by default
returns HTTP 200 with a JSON array of the owner's workspaces
runs 'afc workspace reactivate my-workspace'
calls POST /api/v1/workspaces/my-workspace/reactivate with owner credentials
validates the user API key and confirms the user is the workspace owner
confirms workspace status is 'archived', updates status to 'active' in the workspaces table, sets updated_at
returns HTTP 200 with the updated workspace JSON object (status: active)
prints the workspace JSON to stdout and exits 0

error_handling.condition: Request body is missing or cannot be parsed as JSON
error_handling.behavior: Hub returns HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope {"error":{"code":400,"message":"<descriptive message>"}}

error_handling.condition: Slug fails format, length, start-character, trailing-hyphen, or consecutive-hyphen validation
error_handling.behavior: Hub returns HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope describing the specific slug rule violation

error_handling.condition: git_url is empty, uses plain HTTP, has empty host, or has empty path
error_handling.behavior: Hub returns HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope describing the invalid git_url format

error_handling.condition: branch is provided but violates git ref naming rules
error_handling.behavior: Hub returns HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope describing the invalid branch format

error_handling.condition: org_id is provided but does not reference an existing organization
error_handling.behavior: Hub returns HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope

error_handling.condition: Workspace is active and a delete request is received
error_handling.behavior: Hub returns HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope stating only archived workspaces can be deleted

error_handling.condition: Archive is requested on an already-archived workspace
error_handling.behavior: Hub returns HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope stating the workspace is already archived

error_handling.condition: Reactivate is requested on an already-active workspace
error_handling.behavior: Hub returns HTTP 400 with JSON error envelope stating the workspace is already active

error_handling.condition: Request credential is missing, invalid, or expired
error_handling.behavior: Hub returns HTTP 401 with JSON error envelope; apikit auth middleware handles credential validation

error_handling.condition: Credential is valid but lacks the required permission scope for the endpoint
error_handling.behavior: Hub returns HTTP 403 with JSON error envelope

error_handling.condition: org_id is provided and the creating user is not a member of that organization
error_handling.behavior: Hub returns HTTP 403 with JSON error envelope

error_handling.condition: Workspace slug is not found or exists but credential cannot access it
error_handling.behavior: Hub returns HTTP 404 with JSON error envelope; the 404 is returned in both cases to prevent slug enumeration
Hub returns HTTP 409 with JSON error envelope indicating slug conflict

error_handling.condition: Admin token attempts to create a workspace
error_handling.behavior: Hub returns HTTP 403 with JSON error envelope stating admin tokens cannot create workspaces

error_handling.condition: CLI --confirm flag is omitted from 'afc workspace delete'
error_handling.behavior: afc prints a usage hint to stderr and exits 1 without making any API call

error_handling.condition: CLI --org slug resolution fails (org not found or API returns unexpected shape)
error_handling.behavior: afc prints an error message to stderr and exits 1 without making the workspace create API call
Hub boot sequence returns a non-nil error; the server does not start and does not serve requests

symbols.name: OpenDatabase
symbols.notes: Opens the SQLite database. Hub uses *DB for workspaces table operations.

symbols.name: Bootstrap
symbols.signature: func Bootstrap(ctx context.Context, database *DB, opts BootstrapOptions) error

symbols.name: NewServer
symbols.notes: Hub passes a database-ping HealthChecker implementation.

symbols.name: Server.APIGroup
symbols.notes: Returns the Echo router group for mounting workspace handlers under /api/v1.

symbols.name: Server.MountHandlers
symbols.notes: Hub calls this to register workspace permission scopes (workspaces:read, workspaces:create) and mount handlers.

symbols.name: Permission
symbols.signature: type Permission struct { Resource, Action string }
symbols.notes: Used to declare workspace permission scopes passed to MountHandlers.

symbols.name: RootCommand
symbols.notes: Returns the root CLI command. Hub adds workspace subcommands to this.

symbols.name: LoginCmd
symbols.notes: Standard apikit login command included in afc.

symbols.name: UserCmd
symbols.notes: Standard apikit user management command included in afc.

symbols.name: KeysCmd
symbols.notes: Standard apikit API key management command included in afc.

symbols.name: TokensCmd
symbols.notes: Standard apikit PAT management command included in afc.

symbols.name: OrgsCmd
symbols.notes: Standard apikit organization management command included in afc.

symbols.name: AdminCmd
symbols.notes: Standard apikit admin command included in afc.

symbols.name: CLIPrintError
symbols.notes: Prints a CLI error to stderr.

symbols.name: CLIExitCode
symbols.notes: Converts an error to a process exit code (0 for nil, 1 for non-nil).
